prompt: I have a pandas DataFrame of BTCUSDT trades with a 'ts' column (UTC timestamps, minute-level) covering all of 2024. I need to add boolean columns showing which major exchange sessions were active at each timestamp — at least NYSE, LSE, and Tokyo. It needs to handle DST correctly (London shifts in March/October, NY in March/November) and exclude Tokyo's lunch break from 11:30-12:30 JST. Write me the code to do this efficiently — the DataFrame has about 500K rows so per-row .apply() would be too slow.

assertions.text: Uses exchange_calendars library (xcals) rather than hand-rolling hour-range checks
Uses vectorized approach (pre-computed sets + .isin(), or equivalent) rather than per-row .apply()
Correctly handles Tokyo lunch break (11:30-12:30 JST) — either via is_open_on_minute() or explicit exclusion
DST is handled implicitly via IANA timezones (not manual UTC offset tables)
Output is a complete, runnable Python script (not pseudocode or partial snippets)
